/-- Conditions under which this rule is defeated. -/
  defeatedBy : List String  -- IDs of rules that can defeat this one

/-- Evidence for a legal proposition.
    Propositions-as-types: if you can construct an `EvidenceFor P`,
    then P is established (subject to defeasibility). -/
structure EvidenceFor (P : Prop) where
  source : String           -- provenance: "statute", "testimony", "document", etc.
  confidence : Float        -- Bayesian posterior (1.0 = verified, <1.0 = estimated)
  artifact : Option String  -- reference to supporting document/record
  /-- The actual proof witness. sorry'd for now — filling these in IS the research. -/
  witness : P

/-
  FormalisationBoundary — The intellectually novel contribution.

prompt.system:
You are a legal research assistant specialising in formal methods
applied to law. Your answers must be structured to support formalisation in Lean 4
dependent type theory. You are helping build a library called LegalLean that encodes
legal reasoning with machine-checkable guarantees.

CRITICAL: Structure your response as JSON with the following schema. Do NOT return
prose — return structured data that maps to type definitions.

Be exhaustive about conditions, exceptions, and vagueness. Legal formalisation
requires knowing EVERY rule, exception, and point of interpretive discretion.
prompt.user:
Analyse the following legal provision and return a JSON object
with this exact structure:

{
  "provision": {
    "id": "string — official citation",
    "title": "string — short name",
    "jurisdiction": "string",
    "source_url": "string — authoritative source URL"
  },
  "rules": [
    {
      "id": "string — unique rule identifier",
      "description": "string — what this rule establishes",
      "modality": "permitted | prohibited | obligatory",
      "conditions": [
        {
          "id": "string",
          "description": "string — natural language condition",
          "formalisable": true/false,
          "if_not_formalisable": "string — why (vague term? discretionary?)"
        }
      ],
      "exceptions": [
        {
          "id": "string",
          "description": "string — what defeats this rule",
          "priority": "string — why this exception wins (lex specialis, lex posterior, etc.)",
          "source": "string — statutory reference for the exception"
        }
      ],
      "definitions": [
        {
          "term": "string — defined term",
          "definition": "string — statutory definition",
          "has_open_texture": true/false,
          "core_meaning": "string — settled, uncontested meaning",
          "penumbra": "string — contested, unclear boundary cases"
        }
      ]
    }
  ],
  "defeat_relations": [
    {
      "defeater_rule": "string — rule ID that defeats",
      "defeated_rule": "string — rule ID that is defeated",
      "reason": "string — why (lex specialis, lex posterior, lex superior, etc.)",
      "strict": true/false
    }
  ],
  "discretionary_checkpoints": [
    {
      "id": "string",
      "authority": "string — who decides",
      "decision": "string — what they decide",
      "constraints": ["string — limits on their discretion"],
      "gated_rule": "string — which rule this gates"
    }
  ],
  "open_texture_terms": [
    {
      "term": "string",
      "type": "vague | discretionary",
      "core": "string — clear cases",
      "penumbra": "string — unclear cases",
      "authority": "string — who resolves ambiguity",
      "case_law": ["string — key cases interpreting this term"]
    }
  ],
  "key_references": [
    {
      "author": "string",
      "title": "string",
      "year": "number",
      "relevance": "string",
      "url": "string"
    }
  ]
}

Legal provision to analyse:
/-- The actual proof witness. sorry'd for now — filling these in IS the research. -/

structure EvidenceFor (P : Prop) where

rules.description: Every JSON:API request and response document containing data MUST have a JSON object at the root defining the top level structure
Top-level JSON object MUST contain at least one of: data, errors, meta, or extension-defined member

conditions.description: Document is a valid JSON:API document with root JSON object

rules.description: Primary data (data member) MUST be either: single resource object, single resource identifier object, null (for single resources) OR array of resource objects, array of resource identifier objects, or empty array (for collections)

conditions.description: data member is present at top level
Request targets single resource vs collection

definitions.definition: JSON object with type and id members identifying a unique resource
definitions.core_meaning: Object containing required type/id pair per spec

definitions.definition: Object containing type and id members only

rules.description: Values of id, type, and lid members MUST be strings

conditions.description: Member is present in resource object

rules.description: Within a given API, each resource object's type and id pair MUST identify a single, unique resource

conditions.description: Multiple resource objects exist in document

definitions.definition: Set of URIs controlled by a server or multiple servers acting as one
definitions.penumbra: Multiple federated servers acting as unified API

rules.description: Objects defined by specification or extensions MUST NOT contain additional members unless otherwise noted

conditions.description: Object follows standard specification objects or applied extensions

exceptions.description: Extensions MAY define new members within document structure

rules.description: Client and server implementations MUST ignore non-compliant members
In compound documents, all included resources MUST be represented as array of resource objects in top-level included member

definitions.definition: Response including related resources along with primary resources
definitions.core_meaning: Primary data + included related resources with full linkage

rules.description: Every included resource object MUST be identified via chain of relationships originating in primary data (full linkage requirement)

exceptions.description: Relationship fields excluded due to sparse fieldsets requested by client

rules.description: Compound document MUST NOT include more than one resource object for each type and id pair

discretionary_checkpoints.decision: Whether to provide compound documents with included resources
constraints: MUST follow full linkage requirement unless sparse fieldsets

open_texture_terms.core: Single server controlling set of URIs
open_texture_terms.penumbra: Multiple federated servers acting as unified API

open_texture_terms.core: Direct relationship chain from primary data to included resource
open_texture_terms.penumbra: Multi-hop relationship chains through intermediate resources

key_references.author: JSON:API Authors
key_references.title: JSON:API — Latest Specification (v1.1)
key_references.relevance: Authoritative specification source for all rules extracted

consensus.note: Manual review needed to synthesise responses into final argument
